ksa_text: Skill in testing and validating analytical outputs, including integrated quality assurance testing, operational testing, developing validation procedures, and verifying that analytical results are accurate and reproducible.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: KSA AB-S-009 (Core Abilities/Skill) scores 0.420 composite (highest: EU AI Act at 0.420) and is edge_case for the Data Engineer role. The KSA is an edge case for Core Abilities but contains 0 DE pattern signals sufficient to warrant inclusion for a senior IC data engineer. Seniority is appropriate for IC-to-senior IC data engineering.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No inconsistency identified. Include consistent with edge_case scope, EU AI Act signal (0.420), and Core Abilities handling. Decision stands.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Does market evidence support this Core Ability as a distinct Data Engineer competency at IC/senior IC level?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: General abilities appear implicitly in DE postings but rarely as discrete competencies. When explicit, they appear in context of pipeline design, data modeling, or quality enforcement (DE signals here: 0, composite: 0.420).
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Revised to exclude. General ability without sufficient DE-specific context is not a distinct market requirement. Final decision: exclude.

decision_rationale: Revised to exclude. General ability without sufficient DE-specific context is not a distinct market requirement. Final decision: exclude.
